persistent_rooms.room_description: The Grand Pannonia Hotel lobby is a vast Belle Epoque arrival hall drenched in warm chandelier light. A sweeping staircase rises from the rear center of the room, curving upward with polished dark-wood rails and brass detailing. The floor is patterned marble with soft reflections from the ceiling lights. Reception sits toward the left side with a formal desk for guest check-in, ledger work, and concierge exchanges. Seating clusters and decorative furnishings are arranged around the open floor, but this starting scene should be treated first and foremost as an elegant public hotel lobby rather than a private chamber. The room communicates luxury, old-world stillness, and careful order. No right-side table should be assumed to exist in this lobby description unless a later room-state description explicitly adds one. The scene should preserve period-authentic materials including marble, polished wood, brass, velvet accents, paper stationery, and warm directional light. Suspicious details, if any, should read as subtle mystery elements within an expensive historic hotel rather than horror imagery or modern decor.
